meta.note: Emulator output (15 Oct 2025 OBR model code). The reform is run as in EViews: the policy instrument is overridden in a scenario and solved against a baseline that already carries the OBR's held add-factors, so the impact is scenario − baseline with the model's common drift cancelled. Endpoints are solved; the slider scales linearly between them. Illustrative, not an OBR forecast.

levers.name: Government consumption
levers.desc: Day-to-day government spending — a direct part of GDP. Higher spending lifts demand; the multiplier builds over time.

levers.name: Export demand
levers.desc: External demand for UK exports (e.g. stronger or weaker world growth). Exports add directly to GDP.
